name: Strawberry Trifle
recipeIngredient: 1/3 cup Cream sherry OR orange juice
recipeInstructions:
Custard Sauce:
1. Mix 1½ tablespoons cornstarch with ½ cup milk.
2. Heat 1¾ cups of milk and ¼ cup sugar in a heavy saucepan or double boiler until just to the point of boiling.
3. Remove from heat and stir in the cornstarch mixture until smooth.
4. Cook, stirring constantly, until thickened. It will thicken quickly, so pay attention.
5. Simmer for three minutes, then remove from heat.
6. Beat in 1 teaspoon vanilla extract and 3 beaten egg yolks. Cover and chill.
Trifle:
1. Split the angel loaf cake into 3 layers.
2. Spread jam between the layers and reassemble.
3. Cut the cake into 2-inch cubes.
4. Arrange the cubes in a 2-quart serving bowl and sprinkle with sherry or orange juice.
5. Wash strawberries and pick out 8 to 12 berries for garnish.
6. Remove hulls from remaining berries and slice them.
7. Spoon sliced berries over the cake.
8. Pour chilled Custard Sauce over the berries. Cover and refrigerate for at least an hour.
9. Whip cream to soft peaks, then add powdered sugar and vanilla. Whip until stiff peaks form.
10. Spread whipped cream over custard sauce.
11. Garnish with whole strawberries (green hulls against red and white is pleasing).
12. Sprinkle almonds over the top, if using.
13. Chill before serving. Enjoy within a few hours to prevent sogginess and mushy strawberries.
(TIP): Prepare the Custard Sauce first and refrigerate it until ready for use.
